observations.recommendation:
• Management submit the required documents to the audit team for review.
• Contracts of Service be drawn between the municipal government and the hired job order/contractual personnel in order to establish the extent of responsibilities, work required and other necessary details for the job to be undertaken; and
• The Sangguniang Bayan pass resolution authorizing the hiring of casual and contractual and submit to the audit team.

We have recommended that Management submit a copy of the AIP covering the “Other Special Purpose Appropriations” if any. Henceforth, see to it that all appropriations in the local budget are supported with the required AIP.

We have recommended that the management submit a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement to the audit team in order to verify whether the specific purpose for which the fund was established was met. Henceforth, all transactions which involve funds from other Government Agencies should be fully supported with a Memorandum of Agreement or any appropriate document.

We have recommended that:
• The Municipal Accountant justify the payment of the transactions despite the absence of the BAC Resolution recommending the mode of procurement used and award of the contract.
• The management through the BAC identify the method of procurement used.

We have recommended that Management prepare/submit its supplemental APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes. No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.
observations.reason:
For subitems:
b) Non-conduct of pre-procurement conference - Implemented
c) The BAC recommended the award of project despite contractors/ suppliers failure to submit complete supporting documentations hence; competitiveness and responsiveness of the winning bidders could not be ascertained - Implemented
d) Contractor’s All Risk Insurance was not duly provided for the project. - Not implemented
e) Construction Safety and Health Program duly signed or approved by the Department of Labor and Employment were not submitted by the contractor. - Not implemented
f) Every page of the submitted contract documents does not bear the initial of the BAC members or their duly authorized representatives - Not implemented
g) Posting requirements of Notices of award and Notice to Proceed in the LGU’s PhilGEPS were not adhered; hence, campaign of the government for transparency, accountability and participation was not effectively pursued. - Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management implement only projects set forth in the Annual Investment Program. In instances where other priority projects are identified for implementation, a Sanggunian Bayan resolution should first be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sanggunian Panlalawigan.

We have recommended that management ensure the existence of appropriations before transactions are made or contracts are entered into. In instances where other priority projects are identified for implementation, a Sanggunian Bayan resolution should first be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sanggunian Panlalawigan.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government identify the GAD PPAs through the use of HGDG which would be the basis in the budget determination depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

We have recommended that following:
• Management utilize the GAD fund for programs and projects that will address gender issues;
• The GAD focal person submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in its prescribed format and accompanied with the required reports/documents (cited in item 4.4) to COA to facilitate audit of GAD Fund utilization; and
• The GFPS-TWG Chairperson initiate the strengthening of the Gender and Development Inter-Agency Committee by solidification of its Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System to execute proper determination of GAD objectives and accomplishment of projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer record the audit adjustments in the respective books to correct the erroneous recording of the current year’s transactions.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace back transactions in prior years to determine the causes of the noted discrepancies and adjust accordingly. Henceforth, conduct monthly reconciliation between the accounting, treasury and the bank records to immediately detect errors and prove the correctness and accuracy of their records.

We have recommended that in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/ resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the lacking documents as stated in Annex D by the officials/employees concerned. Henceforth, ensure that claims for terminal leave benefits are completely documented as stated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payments are made.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer locate the missing accountable forms. In case the said accountable forms could not be located, we have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a request for relief of accountability for the loss of accountable forms, with complete documentation, through the Municipal Mayor, addressed to the Audit Team.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to correct the affected accounts for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

the Report on sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to the OCD and

We have recommended Management that in cases of lump sum
appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation

specific purpose that it was created for in consonance with Section 22(c)

We have recommended that management comply with the regulations on
the transfer of unexpended/ unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the
Trust Fund. We have also recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan
Members pass a board resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and
the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate trust fund bank account for

special account and a complete set of subsidiary for the said funds.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government allocate at least
5% of the total budget appropriations as authorized under the General
Appropriations Act for gender and development.

We have recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit GPB to the
PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the
municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and then from
PPDO to the DILG Regional Offices for review and approval and submit
to COA for audit/evaluation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government identify the
GAD PPAs through the use of HGDG which would be the basis in the
budget determination depending on its gender responsiveness or
attribute to GAD interventions.

We recommend that the GAD focal person submit the GAD
Accomplishment Report accompanied with the required
reports/documents (cited in 19.1) and GAD Annual Plan to COA to
facilitate audit of GAD Fund utilization.

We have recommended that management secure the Sanggunian
approval/ resolution and approved GPB before implementing any GAD
infrastructure projects. Funds should be utilized for the specific purpose
it was created.

We have recommended that management establish and/or strengthen
GAD monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and
efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E activities
shall be included in the annual GPB.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to:

submit justification why they should not be held liable for not
deducting/withholding the corresponding tax in its procurement;
deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue
taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its
purchases of goods and services for additional income of the
government and to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the
BIR.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant 																																																																																																																					

effect the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the respective account
balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the
provisions of R.A. No. 9679 to promote the welfare of its officials and
employees. Erring officials submit written justification/s for late/non-
remittance of its withholdings from September to November due to
HDMF as of year-end. Henceforth, remit all contributions and loan
amortizations due to HDMF within the reglementary period to avoid
imposition of sanctions, fines and penalties.

10606 to promote the welfare of its officials and employees.

remittance of its withholdings due to HDMF as of year-end.
Henceforth, remit all contributions and loan amortizations
due to HDMF within the reglementary period to avoid
imposition of sanctions, fines and penalties;
29.11.2 Over-remittance made be collected from the four (4)
municipal officials/employees, and fines/penalties/surcharges
incurred be collected from the erring officials;
29.11.3 The Municipal Accountant - Designate and the Municipal
Treasurer trace back transactions to identify the municipal
official and employees of prior year/s withholdings were due
to properly remit the said premium contributions and loan
amortizations withheld as mandated by laws, rules and
regulations. Interest and surcharges for delayed remittance
shall be borne by the erring officials and employees.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate effect the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the respective account balances in the financial statements. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant - Designate exercise due care in posting and recording transactions.

We have recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes; and direct the concerned department head/personnel to submit justification for the noted lapses. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.

c. Four (4) pakyaw contracts totaling ₱5,328,100.00 include materials of ₱3,492,900.00 and equipment rentals of ₱1,390,000.00.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that pakyaw contracts be awarded to pakyaw labor groups only for the labor component of the project; equipment to be provided by the agency; and the purchases of materials should be through public bidding or any of the authorized modes of alternative procurement.

d. The municipality could have saved the cost for the equipment rentals had it utilized its own equipment for the implementation of an infrastructure project.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management submit an explanation on the reason for including equipment rental in its pakyaw contracts in spite of having the equipment needed for project implementation.

e. One (1) pakyaw contract with a contract cost of ₱4,578,100.00 was awarded to a licensed contractor in violation of Item 3.4, Appendix 1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management submit a valid justification for violating the provision of Item 3.4, Appendix 1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Henceforth, no pakyaw contract shall be awarded to a licensed contractor.

f. All pakyaw contracts totaling ₱5,328,100.00 were awarded to the Pakyaw Group with the lowest bid rate, however, most of the attached proposal forms were not signed by the pakyaw bidder-contractor indicating no competition for the award of pakyaw contracts. In addition, most of the amounts in their bid proposals do not tally with the amount in the abstract of bids.

We have recommended that Management submit justifications on the above noted lapses.

g. Delayed/ Non-submission of Government Contracts precluded the auditorial and technical review of the procurement transactions and prompt detection/ correction of deficiencies.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management furnish the Auditor copy of the contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting documents for auditorial and technical review in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant – Designate take up the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the accounts affected in the Trust Fund for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate should trace back transactions in the previous years in order to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries to reconcile the balances. The Municipal Accountant - Designate should take extra care in recording and posting of transactions involving reciprocal accounts.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to COA complete financial reports, which includes the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts, within the prescribed period as required in PAG 4, PPSAS 1.

We have recommended Management that all financial accounts and reports shall be to the Commission on Audit every 10th day of the following month.  Likewise, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire a qualified Municipal Accountant or assign additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate earlier submission of accounts and reports.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant – Designate take up the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the accounts affected in the Trust Fund for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management exert more effort to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect the collection thereof.  Further, we have recommended that the accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable accounts.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

analyze the entries made in the previous years to identify the causes of the abnormal balances of the above payable accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entries;

submit Journal Entry Vouchers with supporting documents on the reclassification made for the Other Payables account; and

maintain municipal employees index cards in order to avoid excess remittances/ payments/ disbursements.

Further, we have recommended Management to submit a written justification for the over-payments made to the various financial institutions and other creditors.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer locate the missing accountable forms.  In case the said accountable forms could not be located, we have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a request for relief of accountability for the loss of accountable forms, with complete documentation, through the Municipal Mayor, addressed to the Audit Team.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/ resolution must always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable jurisprudence.

We have also recommended that Management should utilize funds for the specific purpose that it was created in consonance with Section 84(1) of P.D. No. 1445 to prevent charges of technical malversation.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Mayor before entering into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government should ensure the existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184, Section 85(1) of PD No. 1445, Section 22 (c) of RA No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence.  We have also recommended that the officer and/or officers entering into contracts be liable to the Government of the Philippines and therefore should refund the contract cost of P2,250,000.00 as if the transaction had been wholly between private contracts in accordance with Section 87 of PD No. 1445.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents on the above noted transactions and ensure that all disbursement vouchers, in future, are properly supported with complete documentation.

We have also recommended that the Supply Officer maintain records of repair for each motor vehicle, as well as, construction and heavy equipment, to determine the frequency of the repairs made and to determine whether the repair to be made is within economic repair.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that pakyaw contracts should be awarded to pakyaw labor groups only, for the labor component of the project; equipment to be provided by the agency; and the purchases of materials should be through public bidding or any of the authorized modes of alternative procurement.

The municipality could have saved the cost for the equipment rentals had it utilized its own equipment for the implementation of an infrastructure project.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management submit an explanation on the reason for including equipment rental in its pakyaw contracts in spite of having the equipment needed for project implementation.

Two (2) pakyaw contracts totaling P398,000.00 were awarded to a licensed contractor in violation of Item 3.4, Appendix 1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management submit a valid justification for violating the provision of Item 3.4, Appendix 1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Three (3) Pakyaw Contracts totaling P750,000.00 were awarded to the Pakyaw Contract with the lowest bid rate but not at the rate as stated in their bid proposals contrary to Appendix 1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that Management submit justifications on the above noted lapses.  All contracts of the Municipal Government should be awarded at the price most advantageous to the Government.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management furnish the Auditor copy of the contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting documents for auditorial and technical review in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.  In addition, we have recommended that Management submit justification/s why the disbursements should be suspended in audit.

We have recommended that the agency officials explain the reasons for resorting to direct contracting as method of procurement for the above items.  We have also recommended that purchases be done through public bidding as mandated under Rule IV Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management prepare its duly approved APP as a guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and submit to COA for audit/evaluation purposes.
We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management establish its PhilGEPS website in order that all its procurement activities will be posted therein for transparency purposes.
